cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which sentence is NOT true based on the information provided about Bobby Moresco. First, I'll go through each option one by one. Option A: His father did not support his work as a bartender. Wait, the text says when he moved to Hollywood, he worked as a waiter. The father's quote is "Stop this craziness and get a job; you have a wife and daughter." So maybe the father didn't support him being an actor, but the text mentions he worked as a waiter, not a bartender. The option says "bar tender" which is different. So perhaps A is incorrect because it mentions a bartender, but Moresco was a waiter. That could be the answer. Option B: Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs. The story shows that he had struggles in the 70s, moved to Hollywood, faced his father's disapproval, then his brother's death, then worked on writing, then Crash. So yes, B is true. Option C: His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets. The text says after his brother's murder in 1983, he started writing to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen. The play is based on his brother's killing. So C is true. Option D: Moresco grew up in Hell's Kitchen which is a few blocks east of Broadway. The text says Hell's Kitchen is next to Broadway.
